title: Chicken and leek pie
subtitle: Creamy chicken and leek filling under an all-butter puff pastry top
excerpt: Chicken and leek pie: bone-in chicken thighs poached until tender, shredded, and folded into a leek and cream sauce, then topped with puff pastry and baked until golden. The poaching liquid becomes the sauce base, so nothing is wasted.
sourceNotes: Chicken pie is a staple of British domestic cookery documented from the seventeenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, includes chicken and white sauce pie recipes. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932), records chicken pie variants from across Britain. The leek-and-cream filling under puff pastry is the modern domestic form, widely reproduced in twentieth-century British baking guides, and is fully public-domain.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked, topping on. Bake from frozen at 200°C fan for 40 to 45 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the filling the day before and refrigerate. Assemble and bake on the day.
recipe.temperatureNote: fan oven, for the pastry top

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, 30 to 35 minutes until pastry is golden

glossaryTerms.term: Using poaching liquid as sauce base
glossaryTerms.definition: When you poach chicken, the liquid absorbs flavour from the bones, skin, and meat. Rather than discarding it, reduce it to concentrate the taste and use it as the liquid base for the sauce. For this pie, the poaching liquid (strained) goes into the roux alongside the cream. The result is a sauce with a depth that plain chicken stock from a carton cannot match. Skim the fat from the surface before using.

content.text: Using bone-in thighs rather than breast meat gives more flavour and a more forgiving texture — the meat can poach for a few minutes over time without going dry. The leeks should be soft and sweet, not squeaky.
Poach the chicken, make the sauce

Place the {{chicken-thigh}} in a saucepan with the {{chicken-stock}} and {{thyme}}. Bring to a simmer and cook for 35 to 40 minutes until the meat pulls away from the bone. Transfer the chicken to a board. Strain and reserve 400 ml of the poaching liquid. See the

note. Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in the same pan over medium heat. Cook the {{leek}} until soft, about 8 minutes. Stir in the {{plain-flour}} and cook for 1 minute. Gradually add the reserved poaching liquid, stirring constantly. Add the {{double-cream}} and simmer until thickened. Season well. Shred the chicken and fold into the sauce. Cool completely.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Spoon the cold filling into a 1.2-litre pie dish. Drape the {{puff-pastry}} over the top, trim, and press the edges to seal. Brush all over with beaten {{eggs}}. Score a small steam hole in the centre. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the pastry is deep gold. Rest for 15 minutes before serving.
